title: Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Abuse Resistance · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for authorization, tenant isolation, and abuse resistance contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Choose role, attribute, relationship, capability, or policy models from resource, action, subject, context, and review needs.
Enforce authorization at every trusted resource and transition boundary using current server-side state.
Propagate tenant identity through query, cache, file, job, event, log, backup, restore, and support paths with adversarial tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Privileged Operation
steps.instruction:
Order the Privileged Operation evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Privileged Operation.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Privileged Operation assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Privileged Operation, keep Privileged Operation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Privileged Operation has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug Privileged Operation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Privileged Operation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug Privileged Operation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.

For Order the regression proof · Debug Privileged Operation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Administrators should have unrestricted standing access.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the debug Privileged Operation case

stimulus.title: Authorization Model case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: An authenticated member changes an identifier, crosses organization data, automates an expensive action, and exploits a standing administrator role. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose role, attribute, relationship, capability, or policy models from resource, action, subject, context, and review needs.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Role-based access control fits every authorization problem.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose role, attribute, relationship, capability, or policy models from resource, action, subject, context, and review needs. Artifact under review: deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite.

options.text: For inspect the failure record, apply Authorization Model without its stated preconditions, omit the deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. The record names the assumption, applies Authorization Model, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Authorization Model record says deny-by-default authorization and abuse case suite looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Authorization Model.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Privileged Operation
steps.instruction:

The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require least privilege, just-in-time or bounded elevation, reason, approval where needed, audit, notification, and revocation for privileged work.
